translation.en: I will go when it rains.

notes:
khi means “when” (at the time that). It connects two actions or situations that happen at the same time or one after another.

It’s a conjunction, so it links two clauses. The action after khi can be in the past, present, or future — the tense depends on context.

Very close to lúc (“when”), which feels a bit more casual in speech. You’ll often see khi in slightly more formal or written Vietnamese.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I will call you when I get home.
I usually drink coffee in the morning when I go to work.
She always feels happy when she meets old friends.

example.en: I will call you when I get home.
example.vi: Tôi sẽ gọi bạn khi tôi về nhà.
example.es: Te llamaré cuando llegue a casa.

definitions.en: A conjunction used to indicate a time or condition.
definitions.vi: Một liên từ được sử dụng để chỉ thời gian hoặc điều kiện.
definitions.es: Una conjunción utilizada para indicar un tiempo o condición.

notes_md:
**khi** means **“when”** (at the time that). It connects two actions or situations that happen at the same time or one after another.

It’s a conjunction, so it links two clauses. The action after *khi* can be in the past, present, or future — the tense depends on context.


Very close to **lúc** (“when”), which feels a bit more casual in speech. You’ll often see *khi* in slightly more formal or written Vietnamese.

content.sentence: Tôi sẽ học ___ có thời gian.

translation.en: I will study when I have time.

exampleSentences: Anh sẽ gọi cho em khi anh về đến nhà.
Em thường uống cà phê vào buổi sáng khi đi làm.
Chị ấy luôn cảm thấy vui vẻ khi gặp bạn bè cũ.
